key_claims: WBAL (W' balance) is your battery for effort distribution over a climb duration; most riders improve climbing speed by distributing effort better rather than just increasing power-to-weight
Negative split climbing strategy (riding the second half faster than the first half) produces faster times than instinctive pacing and can be tested by riding the same climb on consecutive days

Long climbs (15-45+ minutes) reward high fractional utilization driven by mitochondrial density, capillarization, and lactate clearance; neglecting 45-50 minute intervals leads to poor performance on climbs over 15 minutes
Power = torque × cadence; steeper gradients force higher torque at lower cadence, recruiting more muscle fibers and depleting glycogen faster, especially problematic without cadence range training

Fueling 60-90g carbohydrates per hour on long climbs, with pre-climb fueling and 400-800ml hydration before the climb, prevents glycogen depletion and rate of perceived exertion spike; power-to-weight matters less than addressing pacing, engine durability, torque demands, and fueling
specific_numbers: 800 watts for first 6 seconds then 40 watts on 2-minute climb example
2x20 threshold efforts progressing to 3x15 minute efforts
70-72 average cadence starting point progressing to mid-80s
80-120g carbs per hour depending on session intensity
1 kilogram weight reduction mentioned as modest gain
3-4kg weight loss from diet rather than crash diet approach
6-hour Alto Letras climb or 80 kilometers bottom to top
cluster_reasoning: This episode is fundamentally about climbing training methodology (pacing strategy, W' balance distribution, cadence-torque relationships, durability intervals) rather than fueling alone, making coaching the primary cluster; nutrition is secondary because in-ride fueling is explicitly addressed but not the episode's core focus.
